305	  \textit{Source: Kleiman, The Picard Scheme, Exercise (ex:jac).}
306	  Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field $k$. There exists a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $J$ over $k$ of relative dimension $g(C)$ such that, for every $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$, the scheme $J$ is an Albanese object for the pointed curve $(C, P)$ in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:IsAlbanese}. Equivalently, the type of Albanese witnesses for $C$ (Definition~\ref{def:JacobianWitness}) is non-empty.
307	\end{theorem}
308	
309	\begin{proof}
310	  \uses{def:IsAlbanese, def:genus, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:genusZeroWitness, thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
311	  The proof is split into three classical constructions plus the genus-$0$ sub-case absorbed into the same witness (the latter packaged as the explicit construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}, which routes through the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} --- rigidity proved over the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$ --- and then \emph{descends} the resulting morphism equality from $\bar k$ to the base field $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$). Each route produces the same intrinsic scheme $J$; only the universal morphism $\iota_P \colon C \to J$ varies with the marked point $P$. We outline each route and name the Mathlib infrastructure each one requires.
312	
313	  \paragraph{(0) Reduction to a $P$-independent witness.}
314	  The witness $J$ depends only on $C$, not on the marked point $P$. Indeed, both classical constructions below produce $J$ as an intrinsic object of $C$ (the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$, or the Stein-factorisation target of $\Sym^g(C) \to \Pic^g_{C/k}$ translated by a fixed $P_0$); a different choice of $P$ in $C(k)$ alters $\iota_P$ by a translation of $J$, which is an automorphism, and so does not change $J$ as a scheme. The Lean encoding reflects this by bundling $J$ as a single field and the universal-property data as $\forall P,\, \mathrm{IsAlbanese}\, C\, P\, J$. We now turn to producing $J$.
315	
316	  \paragraph{Route A --- Picard scheme.}
317	  This route follows Hartshorne~III.4 and FGA Explained, Chapter~9.
318	
319	  \begin{itemize}
320	    \item[\textbf{(A.1)}] \emph{The relative Picard functor.} For a $k$-scheme $T$, write $C_T := C \times_k T$ and let $\pi \colon C_T \to T$ be the projection. The relative Picard functor is
321	    \[
322	      \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k} \colon (\Sch/k)^{\mathrm{op}} \to \Sets, \qquad
323	      T \longmapsto \Pic(C_T) / \pi^*\Pic(T).
324	    \]
325	    A $T$-point is a line bundle on $C_T$ modulo a line bundle pulled back from $T$. The set-valued formulation suffices for representability; the canonical abelian-group structure on $\Pic(C_T)$ descends to the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ and gives $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ a natural group-functor refinement.
326	
327	    \item[\textbf{(A.2)}] \emph{Representability.} For $C$ smooth proper geometrically connected over $k$ with a $k$-rational point, $\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ (after \'etale sheafification, if $k$ is not algebraically closed) is representable by a $k$-group scheme $\Pic_{C/k}$, locally of finite type. This is FGA's main representability theorem (Grothendieck, FGA~232); the smooth-proper-geometrically-connected hypothesis ensures that the cohomology-and-base-change machinery applies and that $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = k$, which is needed for the quotient by $\pi^*\Pic(T)$ to be \'etale-locally injective.
328	
329	    \item[\textbf{(A.3)}] \emph{The identity component.} Let $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ be the identity component of $\Pic_{C/k}$. As the identity component of a $k$-group scheme locally of finite type, $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is geometrically irreducible; for $C$ smooth proper of genus $g$, it is moreover a smooth proper $k$-group scheme of dimension $g$. Smoothness comes from the deformation-theoretic identification of its tangent space at the identity with $H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)$ and homogeneity of the group action; properness from the fact that the natural map $\Pic^0_{C/k} \to \Pic_{C/k}$ is an open and closed immersion onto a proper component (a consequence of the cohomological criterion: $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ is the kernel of the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$, which is locally constant). Take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$.
330	
331	    \item[\textbf{(A.4)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi universal morphism.} For each $P \in C(k)$, the Abel--Jacobi morphism
332	    \[
333	      \iota_P \colon C \to \Pic^0_{C/k}, \qquad
334	      Q \longmapsto [\mathcal O_C(Q - P)]
335	    \]
336	    sends $P$ to the identity of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$. It is universal among morphisms $f \colon C \to A$ to smooth proper geometrically irreducible $k$-group schemes $A$ with $f(P) = \eta_A$: this is the classical Albanese property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, proved by reducing to the case $k = \overline k$ (Galois descent), where it is a standard consequence of the autoduality of the Jacobian and the fact that line bundles on $C \times A$ trivialised along $\{P\} \times A$ correspond bijectively to morphisms $C \to A^{\vee} = A$ sending $P$ to $\eta_A$.
337	  \end{itemize}
338	
339	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route A.} Each sub-step is missing.
340	  \begin{itemize}
341	    \item Sub-step A.1 requires a working theory of relative line bundles on a product $C \times_k T$ and the descent of the abelian-group structure on $\Pic$ along $\pi^*$. The pieces are present individually but not assembled.
342	    \item Sub-step A.2 requires FGA representability for $\Pic_{C/k}$, which in turn requires Hilbert / Quot scheme infrastructure not in Mathlib (\texttt{HilbertScheme}, \texttt{QuotScheme}, and the cohomology-and-base-change for proper morphisms with finitely presented kernels). The construction engine for this Quot/Hilbert layer is Nitsure, ``Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes'' (FGA Explained; arXiv:math/0504590), \S\,4 (generic flatness and the flattening stratification) and \S\,5 (the construction of $\mathrm{Quot}$, hence $\mathrm{Hilb}$, as a scheme); see \texttt{references/nitsure-hilbert-quot.md}.
343	    \item Sub-step A.3 requires connectedness machinery for group schemes locally of finite type, the identity-component construction $G^0 \subseteq G$, and the degree map $\Pic_{C/k} \to \underline{\mathbb Z}_k$.
344	    \item Sub-step A.4 requires the universal property of $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, which is the classical Albanese functoriality and is not in Mathlib in this form.
345	  \end{itemize}
346	
347	  \paragraph{Route B --- Symmetric powers and Stein factorisation (historical alternative; not pursued).}
348	  \textbf{Iter-144 disposition.} Route B is a historical alternative \emph{not pursued} by the project. It is preserved here for scholarly context only. Route A --- Picard scheme via FGA --- is the committed M3 route per STRATEGY.md \S~M3 iter-144 disposition. The decomposition, gating Mathlib pieces, and midpoint LOC figure ($\sim 9000$ LOC) recorded below are the iter-123 audit values (\texttt{analogies/m3-route-audit.md}); the chapter does not re-cost Route B under current Mathlib snapshots.
349	
350	  This route follows Milne's \emph{Abelian Varieties} (Chapter~III) and avoids FGA representability at the cost of requiring quotients of schemes by finite group actions.
351	
352	  \begin{itemize}
353	    \item[\textbf{(B.1)}] \emph{The $g$-fold symmetric power.} Form the product $C^g$ and let $S_g$ act by permutation of the factors. The categorical quotient $C^{(g)} := C^g / S_g$ exists as a $k$-scheme, smooth and proper over $k$ of dimension $g$. Smoothness follows because the action of $S_g$ on the smooth $k$-scheme $C^g$ is free outside the diagonal locus and the local-ring computation at the diagonal points produces a smooth ring (this is the symmetric-product computation of Fogarty / Mumford). Properness descends from properness of $C^g$ via the geometric-quotient property.
354	
355	    \item[\textbf{(B.2)}] \emph{The Abel--Jacobi morphism on symmetric powers.} Fix a $k$-rational point $P_0 \in C(k)$. The map
356	    \[
357	      \sigma \colon C^{(g)} \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}, \qquad
358	      \sum_{i=1}^g [Q_i] \longmapsto \mathcal O_C\bigl(\textstyle\sum_{i=1}^g Q_i\bigr)
359	    \]
360	    is well-defined (the right-hand side is symmetric in the $Q_i$ and depends only on the unordered tuple) and is surjective. By Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, $\sigma$ is birational onto its image: a generic divisor of degree $g$ on $C$ has $h^0(\mathcal O_C(D)) = 1$, so the fibre $\sigma^{-1}([\mathcal O_C(D)])$ is a point at generic divisors; the special fibres are projective spaces $\mathbb P(h^0 - 1)$ over the locus where $h^0 > 1$.
361	
362	    \item[\textbf{(B.3)}] \emph{The Stein factorisation.} Because $\sigma$ is a proper surjection with geometrically connected fibres (each fibre $\mathbb P^{h^0-1}_k$ is geometrically irreducible), its Stein factorisation
363	    \[
364	      C^{(g)} \xrightarrow{\;\sigma_*\;} \Spec_{\Pic^g_{C/k}}\bigl(\sigma_* \mathcal O_{C^{(g)}}\bigr) \longrightarrow \Pic^g_{C/k}
365	    \]
366	    presents the second arrow as a finite morphism whose source is the intermediate scheme. Since the fibres of $\sigma$ are connected, this intermediate scheme equals $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ scheme-theoretically (the Stein factorisation is trivial on the second arrow when fibres are connected, by Stein's classical theorem). The middle scheme is therefore $\Pic^g_{C/k}$ itself, which is smooth proper geometrically irreducible of dimension $g$. Translating by $-g P_0$ yields $\Pic^0_{C/k}$, and we take $J := \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as in Route A. The universal property is then established as in Sub-step A.4.
367	  \end{itemize}
368	
369	  \emph{Mathlib status for Route B.} Each sub-step is blocked by a different missing piece.
370	  \begin{itemize}
371	    \item Sub-step B.1 requires symmetric powers of schemes (the construction $\Sym^g$ for a $k$-scheme of finite type) and finite-group-scheme quotients of schemes (the quotient $C^g / S_g$). Mathlib has finite-group quotients of types and of various algebraic structures, but not yet a usable scheme-level quotient by a finite group action with the smooth-target property.
372	    \item Sub-step B.2 requires Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch, including the relative version for families of curves --- the cohomology-and-base-change machinery for proper morphisms.
373	    \item Sub-step B.3 requires the Stein factorisation theorem for proper morphisms of locally Noetherian schemes ($f \colon X \to Y$ proper $\implies$ $f_* \mathcal O_X$ is a coherent $\mathcal O_Y$-algebra). Mathlib has partial coherence-theoretic infrastructure but does not yet have the cohomology of $\mathcal O$-modules of finite type along proper morphisms in the form needed.
374	  \end{itemize}
375	
376	  \paragraph{Genus-$0$ sub-case (absorbed into the same witness).}
377	  When $g(C) = 0$ the witness's underlying scheme is $J = \Spec k$, and the universal property reduces to a rigidity statement.
378	
379	  \begin{itemize}
380	    \item[\textbf{(C.1)}] \emph{Brauer--Severi identification.} A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C$ over $k$ with $g(C) = 0$ and a $k$-rational point $P \in C(k)$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_k$. This is the Brauer--Severi triviality criterion: a Brauer--Severi variety of dimension $1$ is $\mathbb P^1$ over its base if and only if it has a rational point. The genus-$0$ identification on the curve side uses $h^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2$ (Riemann--Roch) to produce a degree-$1$ morphism $C \to \mathbb P^1_k$, which is then an isomorphism by degree-and-dimension.
381	
382	    \item[\textbf{(C.2)}] \emph{Rigidity for morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety.} We state the rigidity result over the algebraic closure $\bar k$, since the protected signature of Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} does not assume $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ and the genus-$0$ identification $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ is only available after passing to $\bar k$ (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexamples to a $k$-side identification without a rational point). The conclusion over $k$ --- equality of the given morphism with the constant morphism as $k$-schemes --- then follows by \emph{descent}: a morphism equality that holds after base change to $\bar k$ descends to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f).
383	
384	    \begin{itemize}
385	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.a)}] \emph{Statement (over $\bar k$).} Let $A$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$, with identity element $\eta_A \in A(\bar k)$. Let
386	      \[
387	        f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \longrightarrow A
388	      \]
389	      be a morphism of $\bar k$-schemes such that $f(p) = \eta_A$ for some closed point $p \in \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}(\bar k)$. Then $f$ factors as the composite $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k \xrightarrow{\,\eta_A\,} A$; equivalently, $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$.
390	
391	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.b)}] \emph{Reduction to the project's rigidity lemma.} Let $c \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ denote the constant morphism at $\eta_A$ --- the unique $\bar k$-morphism factoring through the closed-point section $\eta_A \colon \Spec \bar k \hookrightarrow A$. The conclusion $f = c$ follows from Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} applied to $g_1 := f$, $g_2 := c$, $X := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, $Y := A$, once we produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $U.\iota \circ f = U.\iota \circ c$ as scheme morphisms. (After the iter-125 refactor, the cited theorem is stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated; the source-side group-object decoration that the earlier formulation carried has been dropped. $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ satisfies the refactored hypotheses trivially --- it is smooth, hence reduced; it is geometrically irreducible over $\bar k$; and $A$ is proper hence separated over $\bar k$.) The reduction therefore amounts to: produce a non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ on which $f$ and $c$ agree as scheme morphisms; we will in fact take $U = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
392	
393	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.c)}] \emph{Image dimension argument.} The morphism $f$ goes from a proper geometrically integral $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $1$ to a smooth proper $\bar k$-scheme of dimension $g := \dim_{\bar k} A$. The set-theoretic image $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) \subseteq A$ is an irreducible closed subset of $A$ (the continuous image of an irreducible space under a proper map is irreducible and closed) of dimension at most $1$. If $g = 0$ then $A = \Spec \bar k$ and the conclusion is vacuous: the unique $\bar k$-morphism to $\Spec \bar k$ is the constant morphism. Henceforth assume $g \geq 1$, in which case the image is either a single point (the desired conclusion) or a one-dimensional irreducible proper closed subscheme of $A$ --- an irreducible rational curve in $A$.
394	
395	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.d)}] \emph{Key classical input: morphisms from $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ to an abelian variety are constant.} The remaining case --- image of dimension exactly $1$ --- is ruled out by the classical fact that, for an abelian variety $A$ of positive dimension over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$, every morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant. The project ranks three proof routes for this fact; route~(c) is \textbf{committed}, with (b) and (a) as fallbacks.
396	      \begin{itemize}
397	        \item[\textbf{(c) [committed; characteristic-free]}] \emph{Rigidity lemma $+$ theorem of the cube.} Over $\bar k$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}), and every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ to an abelian variety is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); a fortiori the morphism $f$ is constant. The constancy of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ has two ingredients: the \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I --- an elementary closed-map argument, cube-free) supplies the additive/multi-factor structure, while the single-curve base case is supplied by the \emph{theorem of the cube} (\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}), the genuinely deep characteristic-free input forcing that an abelian variety carries no nonconstant image of $\mathbb P^1$ --- the Rigidity Lemma alone does \emph{not} suffice for the base case. Combining these yields the headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality \emph{nor} the Picard representability engine, and is valid in arbitrary characteristic; it is developed in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}. It is the committed route for the genus-$0$ keystone; the over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$) is the fallback-(a) artifact, not consumed here.
398	
399	        \item[\textbf{(b) [fallback]}] \emph{Via the dual abelian variety.} Pullback of line bundles along $f$ induces a homomorphism of group schemes $f^{\vee} \colon A^{\vee} \to \Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$, where $A^{\vee}$ is the dual abelian variety (the moduli space of degree-zero line bundles on $A$). The Picard group of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ has trivial degree-zero component, $\Pic^0(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}) = 0$, because every line bundle on $\mathbb P^1$ of degree $0$ is isomorphic to $\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}$ (Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, II.6 and II.7). Hence $f^{\vee} = 0$. By autoduality of abelian varieties ($A^{\vee\vee} = A$ canonically, double duality of the Poincar\'e line bundle), the zero of $f^{\vee}$ implies that $f$ itself is constant. This route is free once Route~A's $\Pic^0$ engine exists, but it \emph{couples} the genus-$0$ rigidity to the Route~A representability monster, which the committed route~(c) deliberately avoids.
400	
401	        \item[\textbf{(a) [fallback; off the critical path; carries a characteristic-$p$ gap]}] \emph{Via the cotangent bundle (the differential $df = 0$ route).} The cotangent sheaf $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ of an abelian variety is a trivial $\mathcal O_A$-module: the tangent bundle of a group scheme over a field is generated globally by translates of the Lie algebra at the identity, hence is trivial, hence so is its dual. Pulling back along $f$ yields a morphism of locally free sheaves $f^{*} \Omega_{A/\bar k} \to \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}$, whose global sections form a $\bar k$-linear map
402	        \[
403	          H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, f^{*}\Omega_{A/\bar k}\bigr) \longrightarrow H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \Omega_{\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}/\bar k}\bigr) = H^0\bigl(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k},\, \mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(-2)\bigr) = 0.
404	        \]
405	        Triviality of $\Omega_{A/\bar k}$ means the left-hand side is a non-zero $\bar k$-vector space (of dimension $g$); the only way the map can land in zero is for it to be the zero map at every fibre, i.e.\ for the differential $df$ to vanish identically on $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. Smoothness of $A$ over $\bar k$ together with the vanishing of $df$ forces $f$ to be a constant morphism on the geometric points and hence (by reduced-source / separated-target, see C.2.e) as a scheme morphism. This is the \emph{differential} route: it requires the Serre-duality-flavoured vanishing $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = 0$ together with the cotangent-triviality of $\Omega_A$, is retained in the tree via the closed chart-algebra envelope (which supplies only the converse implication $df = 0 \Rightarrow f$ constant), and carries a characteristic-$p$ gap. It is \emph{not} the committed route.
406	      \end{itemize}
407	      Each route yields: $f$ is constant. The pointed condition $f(p) = \eta_A$ then identifies the constant value, so $f = c$ on underlying topological points.
408	
409	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.e)}] \emph{Promoting set-level equality to scheme equality.} The set-level equality $f = c$ on underlying topological points (established in C.2.d) promotes to scheme-morphism equality on any non-empty open $U \subseteq \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via the standard reduced-source / separated-target argument: $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ is reduced (smooth implies regular, regular implies reduced) and $A$ is separated over $\bar k$ (proper implies separated), so a $\bar k$-morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is determined by its values on the underlying topological space. In particular, $f|_U = c|_U$ as scheme morphisms for the open $U := \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ itself, and the reduction of C.2.b gives $f = c$ as scheme morphisms.
410	
411	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.f)}] \emph{Descent of morphism equality from $\bar k$ to $k$.} The rigidity conclusion of Sub-step~C.2.d is proved over the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$, where the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}) carries the hypothesis $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and, being characteristic-free, no $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$. To obtain the corresponding equality of morphisms over the base field $k$, we descend along the base change $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$.
412	
413	      Concretely, let $f, c \colon C \to A$ be two $k$-morphisms (in the application $f$ is the given map and $c$ the constant map at $\eta_A$). Base-change along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ produces $f_{\bar k}, c_{\bar k} \colon C_{\bar k} \to A_{\bar k}$ together with the projections $p \colon C_{\bar k} = C \times_k \bar k \to C$ and $q \colon A_{\bar k} \to A$, fitting the base-change squares $q \circ f_{\bar k} = f \circ p$ and $q \circ c_{\bar k} = c \circ p$. Suppose $f_{\bar k} = c_{\bar k}$ (this is what \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} delivers over $\bar k$). Then $f \circ p = q \circ f_{\bar k} = q \circ c_{\bar k} = c \circ p$, so $f$ and $c$ agree after pre-composition with $p$.
414	
415	      It remains to right-cancel $p$. The morphism $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ is faithfully flat (a field extension is a faithfully flat ring map) and surjective; both properties are stable under base change, so the projection $p \colon C_{\bar k} \to C$ is faithfully flat and surjective. By \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}, $p$ is therefore an epimorphism of schemes, and right-cancelling it in $f \circ p = c \circ p$ gives $f = c$ over $k$. \emph{This closing epi-cancellation is the only genuinely $\sim 2$-line part.} The descent as a whole is \textbf{not} a $\sim 2$-line step but a multi-iteration base-change sub-build: neither this project nor Mathlib provides a base-change functor $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k) \to \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, so even producing $f_{\bar k}, c_{\bar k}$ and the base-change squares $q \circ f_{\bar k} = f \circ p$ above first requires (a) that functor; (b) transfer of every relevant instance along it ($\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,1$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$, $\mathtt{GrpObj}$); (c) stability of $\genus = 0$ under the base change $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ for a geometrically irreducible curve; and (d) the base-change-square commutativity identities. All four must be in place before the closing epi-cancellation applies; only that final cancellation is short, while the surrounding base-change infrastructure is the bulk of the cost.
416	
417	      \item[\textbf{(C.2.g)}] \emph{Mathlib gap statement.} The keystone fact of Sub-step~C.2.d --- that every morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ to a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$ is constant --- is \emph{not} present in current Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) in any form usable here. The project records the committed, characteristic-free form as the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis); the older over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, whose base field still carries both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is retained only as the fallback-(a) artifact. When $g = \dim A = 0$ the statement is vacuous and the declaration's conclusion is automatic. The reduction step C.2.b reuses the project's already-formalised Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (after the iter-125 refactor, this is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated); the promotion step C.2.e uses standard reduced-source / separated-target lemmas, partially in Mathlib. 444	\label{sec:genusZeroWitness}

448	\subsection{Route (c): rigidity via the theorem of the cube (committed genus-$0$ proof route)}
449	\label{sec:av_rigidity_route_c}
450	
451	The committed genus-$0$ route~(c) --- the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack --- is now developed in its own chapter, \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, which sits \emph{upstream} of this chapter in the import graph: it covers \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean}, which imports \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Genus} and is imported by \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian}. This relocation is precisely what breaks the former \texttt{RigidityKbar}\,$\to$\,\texttt{Rigidity}\,$\to$\,\texttt{Jacobian} import cycle, so that \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} can consume the keystone directly. The genus-$0$ arm consumes that chapter's headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} --- the characteristic-free replacement (\emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis) for the over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, which remains in tree as the fallback-(a) artifact and still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$.
452	
453	The chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} has four links. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I) is an elementary properness/closed-map argument --- cube-free and cohomology-free --- and supplies the additive/multi-factor structure. The \emph{theorem of the cube} (\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}) enters as the genuinely deep, characteristic-free input supplying the single-curve base case that an abelian variety carries no nonconstant image of $\mathbb P^1$; the cube is \textbf{required} here --- the Rigidity Lemma alone does \emph{not} establish that base case. Combining the two gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the genus-$0$ classification $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ over $\bar k$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}) yields \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality \emph{nor} the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is fully \textbf{decoupled} from Route~A; the differential / Serre-duality argument survives only as the off-critical-path fallback~(a) of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}.

455	\begin{theorem}\leanok
456	[Genus-$0$ Albanese witness]
457	  \label{def:genusZeroWitness}
458	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}
459	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV, def:genus}
460	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is $\Spec k$ (the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $0$, properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$ field encodes the Albanese universal property for every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$.
461	\end{theorem}
462	
463	\begin{proof}
464	  \uses{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:IsAlbanese, def:JacobianWitness, def:genus}
465	  We construct the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness}.
466	
467	  \paragraph{Underlying scheme.} Take $J := \mathbf{1}$, the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, which on underlying schemes is $\Spec k$ viewed as a $k$-scheme via the identity structure morphism.
468	
469	  \paragraph{Group-object structure, properness, smoothness, geometric irreducibility.} The terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ of any cartesian-monoidal category carries the (unique) trivial group-object structure: multiplication and inverse are the unique morphisms to $\mathbf{1}$, and the identity element is the identity morphism. The structure morphism $\mathbf{1}.\mathtt{hom} = \mathrm{id}_{\Spec k}$ is proper (the identity is a closed immersion, in particular proper), smooth (the identity is unramified and flat, hence smooth), and geometrically irreducible. The geometric-irreducibility instance is the project-local helper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.geometricallyIrreducible\_id\_Spec} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:120}--\texttt{126}), which derives geometric irreducibility of the identity on $\Spec k$ from the fact that, on the geometric fibre $\Spec K$ over any field extension $k \to K$, the second projection of the pullback square is an isomorphism, and $\Spec K$ is irreducible.
470	
471	  \paragraph{Smoothness of relative dimension $\genus\,C$.} The smoothness witness $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is required at relative dimension $\genus\,C$. The hypothesis $\genus\,C = 0$ rewrites the target relative dimension to $0$, after which the goal reduces to $\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 0\ (\mathrm{id}_{\Spec k})$. This is a Mathlib instance: the identity morphism on any base is smooth of relative dimension $0$.
472	
473	  \paragraph{The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field.} For every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$, we exhibit an \cref{def:IsAlbanese} term for the triple $(C, P, J)$.
474	
475	  The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map $\alpha := \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \colon C \to \mathbf{1}$ (the structure morphism of $C$ as a $k$-scheme, factored through the terminal object). The pointed condition $P \circ \alpha = \eta_J = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}$ holds automatically: both sides are morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, and the terminal object has a unique endomorphism.
476	
477	  For the universal factorisation: given a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $A$ over $k$ with identity element $\eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$ and a morphism $f \colon C \to A$ with $P \circ f = \eta_A$, we must exhibit a unique morphism $g \colon J \to A$ such that $f = \alpha \circ g$. \emph{Existence}: take $g := \eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$. Then $\alpha \circ g = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$, which equals $f$ precisely when $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. We obtain this equality by descent from the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$. Base-change the data along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$: the curve $C_{\bar k}$ is again smooth proper geometrically irreducible of genus $0$ (genus is stable under base change for a geometrically irreducible curve), the group scheme $A_{\bar k}$ is again smooth proper geometrically irreducible, the base-changed point $P_{\bar k}$ is a $\bar k$-rational point, and $f_{\bar k}$ still kills it: $f_{\bar k}(P_{\bar k}) = \eta_{A_{\bar k}}$. By \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (the project's characteristic-free genus-$0$ rigidity keystone of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis; it lives in the upstream \texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean}, imported by \texttt{Jacobian.lean}, so consuming it here introduces no import cycle), $f_{\bar k}$ equals the constant morphism $\mathtt{toUnit}\,C_{\bar k} \circ \eta_{A_{\bar k}}$ over $\bar k$. Finally we descend this morphism equality back to $k$: the projection $p \colon C_{\bar k} \to C$ is the base change of the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$, hence faithfully flat and surjective, hence an epimorphism of schemes by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}; right-cancelling $p$ against the base-change-square identities yields $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$ as $k$-scheme morphisms (Sub-step~C.2.f of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}). \emph{Uniqueness}: if $g_1, g_2 \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$ both satisfy $f = \alpha \circ g_i$ with $\alpha = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C$, then $\alpha \circ g_1 = \alpha \circ g_2$, and we right-cancel $\alpha$. This is the \emph{same} epi-cancellation as in the Existence paragraph: the underlying scheme morphism of $\alpha$ is $C.\mathtt{hom} \colon C \to \Spec k$ (\texttt{Over.toUnit\_left}), which is faithfully flat and surjective (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve is faithfully flat and surjective over its base field), hence an epimorphism of schemes by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}; therefore $\alpha$ is an epimorphism in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ by \texttt{Over.epi\_of\_epi\_left}, and \texttt{cancel\_epi} gives $g_1 = g_2$. Uniqueness does \emph{not} follow from terminality of $\mathbf{1}$: morphisms \emph{out} of a terminal object are global points and are not unique in general (terminality gives uniqueness only of morphisms \emph{into} $\mathbf{1}$).
478	
479	  \paragraph{Vacuity for the $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch.} When no $k$-rational point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$ exists (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the universal quantifier $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\,C\,P\,J$ is vacuously satisfied at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall$ over an empty type is trivially true. The construction handles this branch implicitly without any explicit case split.
480	
481	  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of the upstream keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (M2.a; in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean}, imported by \texttt{Jacobian.lean}), whose \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) --- the characteristic-free rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, which is decoupled from both Route~A representability and Serre duality. (The over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} is the retained fallback-(a) artifact, not the keystone consumed here.) The differential decomposition into pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}) is the retained \emph{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path. (The chart-algebra envelope of the fallback supplies only the converse implication $df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constant, and carries a characteristic-$p$ gap; the committed route~(c) avoids that gap entirely.)
482	\end{proof}

489	\begin{theorem}\leanok
490	[Positive-genus Albanese witness]

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$, where $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}$ denotes the \emph{presheaf-level} inverse-image functor (left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$).
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies that on every affine chart $V \to U$ (with $V \subseteq X$, $U \subseteq S$ affine and $f V \subseteq U$) the appLE-algebra K\"ahler differential $\Omega_{\Gamma(X, V)\,/\,\Gamma(S, U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank via the same forward implication. The bridge between this affine-chart-wise algebra-K\"ahler form and the section module of the relative cotangent presheaf $\Omega_{X/S}$ was the former content of milestone~M1; the bridge declaration was excised iter-126 (zero in-tree consumers) and replaced in this chapter by the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}, the off-loop Mathlib-PR work of which proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}. The converse implication of the forward smoothness criterion is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The presheaf-level inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S}$ on $X$ (the left Kan extension of $\struct{S}$ along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$, given on each open $V \subseteq X$ by the colimit $\mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$) is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) \,/\, (f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  Here $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V) = \mathrm{colim}_{W : \,f V \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$ is the presheaf-level inverse-image section ring, \emph{not} $\struct{S}(U)$ for an arbitrary affine open $U \supseteq f V$. The relationship between this colimit-source K\"ahler module and the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$ for $A = \struct{S}(U)$, $B = \struct{X}(V)$ on an affine chart $V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}U$ with $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$ affine is the content of the bridge documented in Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The colimit description of $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$ is the definition of \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback} as the left Kan extension along $(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,f.\mathrm{base}).\mathrm{op}$ in Mathlib.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} — but for the \emph{colimit-source} ring $(f^{-1}_{\mathrm{psh}}\struct{S})(V)$, not for $\struct{S}(U)$. Transferring to the classical $A = \struct{S}(U)$ source on an affine chart is the bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on the relative K\"ahler differentials: every point of $X$ admits an affine chart $V \to U$ over which the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is free of the prescribed rank, with $A = \struct{S}(U)$ and $B = \struct{X}(V)$. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. We state and prove the theorem directly on the appLE-algebra K\"ahler module, sidestepping the presheaf bridge of Section~\ref{sec:bridge}. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free (algebra-K\"ahler form)]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exist an affine open $U \subseteq S$, an affine open $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \subseteq f^{-1}(U)$, such that the K\"ahler differential module
54	  \[
55	    \Omega_{\Gamma(X, V) \,/\, \Gamma(S, U)}
56	  \]
57	  (over the appLE algebra structure $\Gamma(S, U) \to \Gamma(X, V)$ induced by $f$) is a free $\Gamma(X, V)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix $x \in X$. We use the abbreviation $A := \Gamma(S, U)$ and $B := \Gamma(X, V)$ for the section rings on the affine chart we will produce; the algebra map $A \to B$ is the appLE map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}$ induced by $f$.
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $U \subseteq S$ and $V \subseteq X$ with $x \in V$ and $V \leq f^{-1}U$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,U\,V\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Step 4.5: discharge the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ side condition.} The chart witness $x \in V$ shows that the open $V \subseteq X$ is non-empty as a set, so its structure-sheaf section ring $B = \Gamma(X, V) = \struct{X}(V)$ is non-zero by \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (the structure sheaf of a scheme has \texttt{Nontrivial} sections on a non-empty open). This supplies the \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ hypothesis required by Step 4.
90	
91	  Combining Steps 1--4.5, the chart $(U, V, e)$ produced by Step 1 satisfies $x \in V$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U$, $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V$, and under the appLE algebra structure $A = \Gamma(S, U) \to B = \Gamma(X, V)$, the K\"ahler module $\Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	
93	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
94	
95	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
96	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
98	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
99	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
100	  and \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.component\_nontrivial} (Step 4.5).
101	\end{proof}
102	
103	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
104	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
105	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
106	\end{remark}
107	
108	\section{Standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities (post-iter-126 M1 excise)}
109	\label{sec:bridge}
110	
111	The presheaf-form $\Omega_{X/S}$ of Definition~\ref{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf} and the algebra-K\"ahler form $\Omega_{B / A}$ on an affine chart of Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} are connected, on an affine chart, by a canonical $B$-linear isomorphism. Earlier iterations of the project (iter-121 through iter-125) hosted this isomorphism in the Lean tree as a \emph{bridge} declaration \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_equiv\_kaehler\_appLE} backed by a Mathlib gap-fill sub-lemma \texttt{appLE\_isLocalization} (the load-bearing localization-at-the-image-submonoid claim, packaged as a multi-LOC residual sorry whose closure was concrete but had no in-tree consumer). The forward-direction smoothness criterion (Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}) closed iter-120 in algebra-K\"ahler form \emph{independently} of the bridge, so the bridge was project-local infrastructure with zero downstream consumer.
112	
113	\textbf{Excise decision (iter-126).} Per the iter-126 strategy-critic CHALLENGE, ratified by the iter-126 user hint endorsing "shortest path qualified by your role is to fill mathlib gaps", the bridge declaration and its load-bearing sub-lemma were excised from the Lean tree iter-126: closing the bridge produced neither a downstream consumer nor a Mathlib PR (the extractable PR candidate is M1.d — Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso} below — and is \emph{independent} of the bridge). The two retained declarations in this section are the standalone K\"ahler-localization utilities that survive as upstream-PR-quality material; off-loop PR work proceeds from \texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}.
114	
115	\begin{lemma}\leanok
116	[K\"ahler module of a localization is trivial]
117	  \label{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
118	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
119	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization at a multiplicative subset $M \subseteq A$. Then $\Omega_{L / A}$ is subsingleton (the zero $L$-module).
120	\end{lemma}
121	
122	\begin{proof}
123	
124	\leanok
125	  This is a thin re-export of Mathlib's existing infrastructure: \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~\texttt{[verified]} (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic}, line 303) produces the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified}~$A$~$L$ from any \texttt{IsLocalization}~$M$~$L$ predicate; the instance \texttt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton\_kaehlerDifferential}~\texttt{[verified]} (same file, lines 57--59) then yields \texttt{Subsingleton}~$(\mathrm{KaehlerDifferential}~A~L)$. Hence the project-side lemma is a two-line re-export ($\mathtt{haveI} := \mathtt{Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of\_isLocalization}~M$ \texttt{; infer\_instance}); contra an earlier framing, no Mathlib gap-fill is required for this lemma.
126	\end{proof}
127	
128	\begin{lemma}\leanok
129	[Tower-cancellation equivalence for K\"ahler modules under localization]
130	  \label{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
131	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}
132	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
133	  Let $A \to L$ be a localization (in the sense of \texttt{IsLocalization}), and let $L \to B$ be a ring map forming a scalar tower $A \to L \to B$. Then the canonical $B$-linear map $\Omega_{B/A} \to \Omega_{B/L}$ induced by the tower is a $B$-linear equivalence.
134	\end{lemma}
135	
136	\begin{proof}
137	  \uses{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}
138	  \leanok
139	  The second fundamental exact sequence of K\"ahler differentials reads
140	  \[
141	    B \otimes_{L} \Omega_{L/A} \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/A} \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \Omega_{B/L} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
142	  \]
143	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton}, the left term vanishes (since $\Omega_{L/A}$ is subsingleton and tensoring against it gives the zero module). Therefore the right surjection has zero kernel and is a $B$-linear equivalence. The Mathlib closure pieces are \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map}~\texttt{[verified]} (the exact sequence above) and \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.map\_surjective}~\texttt{[verified]} (the right-hand surjection), both in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}; the project-side declaration packages these into a \texttt{LinearEquiv} via \texttt{LinearEquiv.ofBijective}. This is the most extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone~M1: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case, candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}.
144	\end{proof}
145	
146	\begin{remark}[Mathlib contribution candidate (preserved standalone post-excise)]
147	  \label{rem:bridge_mathlib_pr}
148	  Per the analogist's analysis (\texttt{analogies/relative-differentials-presheaf-bridge.md}, iter-121), the extractable Mathlib contribution candidate is Lemma~\ref{lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso}: a clean generalisation of \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale} to the ``only the lower step is formally unramified'' case (candidate name \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified}, candidate Mathlib home \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic}). This lemma survives the iter-126 M1 excise as standalone PR-ready material; the bridge declaration and its scheme-morphism-shaped \texttt{appLE\_isLocalization} sub-lemma were excised iter-126 because they had zero in-tree consumers, but the Mathlib-PR work proceeds off-loop from the analogist's file.
149	\end{remark}
150	
151	\section{Converse direction (milestone M4)}
152	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
153	
154	This direction is recorded as an optional future milestone~M4 (see \texttt{STRATEGY.md}). Closure requires the deformation-theoretic hypothesis \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)}, which the project may wire up if a downstream consumer demands the iff form. The remainder of this section documents the obstruction in concrete form: a counterexample showing that without that hypothesis the converse fails, and a comparison with the Mathlib converse lemma at the ring level.
155	
156	The reverse implication
157	\[
158	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
159	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
160	\]
161	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
162	
163	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
164	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
165	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
166	\end{remark}
167	
168	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
169	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
170	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
171	  \[
172	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
173	  \]
174	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
175	  \begin{itemize}
176	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
177	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
178	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
179	  \end{itemize}
180	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is the content of milestone~M4.
181	\end{remark}
182	
183	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
184	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
185	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
186	\end{remark}
187	
188	\section{Content scheduled for later milestones}
189	
190	Each bullet below is a future milestone whose Mathlib infrastructure is not yet available in the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227}; inclusion in this list does \emph{not} mean the project has abandoned it. The roadmap progression is: M1 (this iter) $\to$ M2 / M3 (existence routes) $\to$ M4 (converse, Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}) $\to$ M5+ (sheafification, cotangent exact sequence, etc., listed here). Each item lists the deferred concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
191	
192	\begin{itemize}
193	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (M5).} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is the deferred work for this milestone. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
194	
195	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence (M6).} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
196	  \[
197	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
198	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
199	  \]
200	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
201	
202	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section (M7).} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
203	
204	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity (M8).} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
205	  \[
206	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
207	  \]
208	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot yet be expressed within the project's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
209	\end{itemize}
210

# Blueprint Review Report

## Slug
iter163

## Iteration
163

## Top-level summaries

### Incomplete parts
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:hom_additivity_over_product` (Milne Cor 1.5 — THE intended
  next prover target): the proof writes the decomposition `h = f∘p + g∘q` and the difference
  `φ = h − (f∘p + g∘q)` using `+`/`−` on morphisms into `A` freely, but never says where the
  **abelian-group structure on `Hom(V×W, A)`** comes from. In the Lean encoding `A` is `[GrpObj A]`
  only (verified: `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:801,852` — *not* `CommGrpObj`). The "+" decomposition
  and its uniqueness presuppose `A` commutative (Milne's Cor 1.4, itself a rigidity consequence), or
  at minimum that the pointwise Hom-monoid is a commutative group. The math is faithful to Milne;
  the *formalizable detail a prover needs* (which instance supplies the commutative-group `+` on
  morphisms-into-`A`) is missing. This is the crux of the Lean work and should be spelled out before
  the prover runs.

### Proofs lacking detail
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `lem:hom_additivity_over_product`: as above — add one sentence
  naming the source of the abelian-group structure on `Hom(−, A)` (commutativity of `A` / a
  `CommGrpObj` instance / Milne Cor 1.4), and how `f∘p + g∘q` is built in Lean (`GrpObj` mul ∘ lift).

### Lean difficulty quality
- `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity}` and
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero}`: both are `[expected]` (not yet in the Lean
  file — confirmed absent). The *statements* are well-formed targets, but the return type implicitly
  needs the commutative-group structure on morphisms into `A` (see above). Recommend the writer pin
  this so the prover infers the right type rather than guessing.

### Citation discipline
